note: blocked: no MoE/router in baseline; SIMBAL is an MoE-router Gram orthogonality regularizer (arXiv:2506.14038) — the 'router weight matrix' it regularizes doesn't exist in this repo (zero hits for moe/expert/router in configs/, models/, training/); adding MoE infrastructure is >200 LoC and infeasible at tiny1m3m's 0.94M params; needs-taste review missed the tier/infrastructure mismatch

blocked again: no MoE/router in baseline repo. SIMBAL (arXiv:2506.14038) regularizes a router W_r via ||W_r^T W_r/d - I||^2_f, but this repo has zero MoE/expert code (no hits in models/, configs/, training/, optimizers/). Implementing MoE from scratch is >200 LoC and parameter-infeasible at tiny1m3m's 0.94M budget. Needs-taste should pick an MoE-friendly paper or a non-MoE design that fits the existing dense transformer.

blocked (3rd time, same root cause): SIMBAL is an MoE-router orthogonality regularizer (||W_r^T W_r/d - I||^2_f on the d x n_experts router matrix) — this repo is a dense-transformer training kit with zero MoE/expert code (verified: no hits for moe|expert|router in configs/, models/, training/, optimizers/). Paper validates at 762M-active / 3.14B-total MoE; tiny1m3m's 0.94M param budget cannot fit even a 2-expert MoE without blowing the 5% ctrl-budget ceiling. A creative re-purposing onto a dense FFN matrix would not test SIMBAL's hypothesis (similar inputs → similar experts) — it would test 'decorrelated FFN columns', a different mechanism. needs-taste should pick an MoE-friendly paper or a non-MoE orthogonality-orthogonal mechanism (e.g. weight orthogonality pen on FFN up-proj, or a SIMBAL-style Gram pen on the residual stream) and write a new idea.md.
